_comment: AGNI hub configuration optimized for Raspberry Pi village server (60 students, Android Marshmallow edge devices)
nodeVersionNote: Raspberry Pi OS Bullseye ships Node 12 by default. Upgrade to Node 18+ before deploying; Node 12 will cause silent failures.
hubIdNote: Replace CHANGE_ME with a unique per-hub ID before deploy. Duplicate hubIds corrupt sync attribution and USB filenames.
capacityNote: Memory budget assumes maxStudents and maxLessons. Runtime enforced: engine and accounts reject when at cap.
_path_notes: Paths above are absolute for dedicated Pi deployment. No fallback: if dataDir/serveDir/yamlDir do not exist, hub startup fails with ENOENT at first file access.
Before first start: create dirs and run init:data. Example: sudo mkdir -p /opt/agni/data /opt/agni/serve /opt/agni/lessons && AGNI_DATA_DIR=/opt/agni/data node scripts/init-data.js
Permissions: hub process user needs rwx on dataDir, serveDir, yamlDir; r on factoryDir, katexDir.
Failure mode when dataDir missing: process throws ENOENT (no such file or directory) on first read/write (accounts, LMS state, mastery-summary, etc.). Run init:data first.
_engine_notes: embeddingDim=8 (above): Halves the default (16) to reduce matrix sizes.
- Bandit A matrix: 16x16 instead of 32x32 (4x less memory and compute).
- Cholesky inversion: O(16^3)=4096 vs O(32^3)=32768 FLOPs per selection.
- Embedding vectors: 8 floats per student/lesson instead of 16.
forgetting=0.96 (above): Slightly more aggressive forgetting for small cohorts. Valid range [0.5,1]; values outside corrupt bandit decay.
With 60 students, the bandit accumulates evidence slowly, so faster
forgetting keeps the model responsive to changing student performance.
hubId (above): Must be unique per hub. If copying this config to a second Pi, change it.
maxStudents/maxLessons (above): Budget assumes these limits. Runtime enforced.
Memory budget for 60 students, 200 lessons (embeddingDim=8, featureDim=16):
Rasch students: 60 × 16 B = 960 B (ability+variance, 2×float64 per RaschStudentState)
Rasch probes: 200 × 24 B = 4.7 KB
Embeddings (students): 60 × 8 floats × 8 B = 3.8 KB
Embeddings (lessons): 200 × 8 floats × 8 B = 13 KB
Bandit A (16×16 float64, featureDim=embeddingDim×2): 2.0 KB
Bandit b (16): 128 B
Markov transitions (sparse): ~50 KB typical
Markov history (60 students × 10): ~5 KB
Total LMS state: ~80 KB on disk (JSON).
In-memory: engine uses plain Array, not TypedArray — V8 object overhead
(hidden classes, property descriptors) can make in-RAM 3–5× raw bytes.
Expect ~0.6–1 MB for LMS objects; still fine on Pi 3B+ (1 GB).
Total Node.js process: ~30–50 MB RSS
